response_text:
# Thule Group (THULE.ST) — Initial Note

**Thesis.** Thule is a high-quality consumer discretionary brand (roof racks, bike carriers, strollers) that has de-rated meaningfully over the past year. A board-level insider has just put ~22 MSEK of personal capital into the stock across three buys in eight days at current levels — that is an unusually concentrated and sizeable signal worth taking seriously on a Mid Cap name.

**Positives**
- Three insider purchases by Curt Blomquist totaling ~22 MSEK in 8 days; this is meaningful personal capital, not symbolic.
- Price has stabilized over 3 months (+0.4%) after a -12.4% 12-month drawdown — drawdown phase may be exhausting.
- Thule is a recognized premium brand with structurally decent margins historically; sector is cyclical but the franchise is durable.
- Trading 22% below 52-week high — entry is at a depressed level, not chasing strength.

**Concerns**
- I cannot see current earnings, margins, debt, or guidance from the data available — the de-rating may reflect genuine fundamental weakness (consumer slowdown, inventory destocking) that I cannot verify or quantify.
- Consumer discretionary is cyclical; if Europe/US consumer weakens further, Thule earnings are exposed.
- 30-day vol of 36.8% is elevated for a Mid Cap — market is unsettled on this name.
- One insider, however large, is still one data point; I'd want to see if others follow.

**Insider read.** Strong positive signal. ~22 MSEK across three trades in eight days at ~226 SEK by a single individual is concentrated conviction. On Mid Cap, this is the kind of insider pattern that historically rewards attention. Not a guarantee — but it shifts the prior.

**Price read.** 226 SEK is -4.3% below 200-day MA, -22% from 52w high, and the 3-month change is flat. This looks like a base-building level rather than a falling knife, though vol remains high.

**Verdict.** `INTERESTING — fits Core sleeve`

The insider signal is the trigger; the brand quality justifies Core classification. PM should size cautiously given I have no visibility into current fundamentals — this is a "follow the insider, verify the fundamentals later" setup, so a starter position rather than full size is appropriate.
